text.title: rust-ownership Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-ownership as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Keeping an immutable reference live while attempting a mutable borrow of the same value.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The compiler rejects this to prevent data races. Restructure so the shared borrow ends before the mutable one begins.

failure-mode.description: Using Rc or RefCell in async code that is sent to tokio::spawn.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rc and RefCell are not Send/Sync. tokio::spawn requires 'static + Send. The compile error is a correct diagnosis: redesign ownership with Arc.

failure-mode.description: Multiple overlapping borrow_mut() calls on the same RefCell across await points.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: borrow_mut() panics at runtime if already borrowed. Unlike compile-time borrow errors, these surface only under specific execution paths in production.

failure-mode.description: A struct field that borrows from another field of the same struct (self-referential).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Not expressible in safe Rust without Pin + unsafe or crates like ouroboros. Agents write these confidently and produce "cannot borrow as mutable" or "borrowed value does not live long enough" errors. Redesign to hold owned data or use indices instead of references.

failure-mode.description: Capturing a reference to a local variable inside tokio::spawn(async move { ... &ref ... }).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: tokio::spawn requires 'static. Local references are not 'static. The "fix" of adding 'static to the type is wrong — use Arc::clone to share ownership instead.

failure-mode.description: Adding .clone() to silence a borrow-checker error without understanding the lifetime conflict.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gratuitous clones double allocation, hide architectural problems, and mask bugs that the borrow checker was correctly diagnosing. Force agents to articulate the lifetime conflict before applying any clone.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'static bounds to structs, function parameters, or generics "to make it compile".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 'static means the type must live for the entire program. Adding it to an API parameter forces every caller to provide a leaked or globally-static value. Use Arc instead.
